propositions: Release workflow references first-party actions by mutable major-version tags instead of full-SHA commit pins, so identical source SHAs can execute different build/publish code.
Release checkouts do not set persist-credentials: false, leaving the token persisted in local git config.

Build tools install with unrestricted upgrades and no hash lock; a hash-locked constraints file installed via --require-hashes plus --no-isolation is required to govern the Hatchling and build-tool versions.

The MCP publisher is downloaded from a mutable releases/latest URL and executed without digest/signature verification in a job holding id-token: write, requiring an exact-version pin, SHA-256 verification, and a structural split into unprivileged mcp_verify and OIDC-privileged mcp_registry jobs.

Generic action-security checks (full-SHA pinning, credential persistence, over-broad OIDC permissions) are enforced by zizmor and actionlint under make lint scoped to release.yml, with one annotated excessive-permissions suppression on mcp_registry.
Release evidence must be captured via per-job distinctly-named fragments concatenated by a consolidation job into release-evidence.txt, since artifacts are job-scoped and non-appendable.
